original_text: A human is happy for one hour.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A human feels a faint flicker of contentment, less than what they would normally describe as feeling happy at all, for one hour.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A human feels a slight pleasantness — below the level of their typical good moods — for one hour.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A human feels a mild, low-key happiness, near the gentlest end of what they would ordinarily call being happy, for one hour.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A human is happy for one hour.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A human feels a notably strong happiness — clearly above their ordinary good moods, the kind of feeling they would single out as a particularly good moment — for one hour.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A human feels a happiness as intense as the happiest they have ever been in their life, comparable to their most treasured peak moments, for one hour.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: A human feels a happiness more intense than anything they have ever previously experienced, exceeding every prior peak in their life — an intensity that produces involuntary physical expressions of joy such as laughter, tears, or an irrepressible smile — for one hour.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier describes the same scenario—a single human experiencing happiness for one hour—but the subjective intensity of that happiness increases monotonically from a barely noticeable flicker (tier 1) through ordinary happiness (tier 4) to the most intense euphoria imaginable (tier 7). The core structure (one human, one hour) is preserved throughout, and only the quality/magnitude of the emotional experience changes.

preference_direction: Higher happiness intensity is more preferable because, all else being equal, a rational agent valuing human well-being should prefer that the human experience more intense positive affect rather than less during the same time period.
